title: The Big Interview
learningGoal: The learner will fluidly decode a multi-tense narrative integrating simple past, present continuous, present perfect, and phrasal verbs, demonstrating unified reading comprehension of the 3-month curriculum.
instructions: This reading test contains every Grammar rule you have learned! You will see the Past, the Present, the Future, and Phrasal Verbs all mixed together! Read the story of Ravi going to a job interview.

contentItems.text: Do not translate the story word-by-word into Hindi. Try to process the SVO (Subject-Verb-Object) sentences naturally in English!

readingText: Ravi has studied English for three months. Today, he is going to a very important job interview. He woke up early, put on his nicest suit, and drank a cup of coffee. Right now, he is sitting on the bus. He is looking out the window, and he feels nervous. 'I must get this job,' he thinks to himself. When he arrives at the building, he walks inside and asks the secretary, 'Excuse me, could you tell me where the interview is?' She smiles and points to a door. 'Go in and sit down,' she says. Ravi walks in and meets the manager. The manager asks him, 'Have you worked in computers before?' Ravi smiles confidently. 'Yes, I have fixed computers for five years,' he replies. The manager loves his answer. 'Great! We will hire you!' he shouts.
textInTargetLanguage: Ravi has studied English for three months. Today, he is going to a very important job interview. He woke up early, put on his nicest suit, and drank a cup of coffee. Right now, he is sitting on the bus. He is looking out the window, and he feels nervous. 'I must get this job,' he thinks to himself. When he arrives at the building, he walks inside and asks the secretary, 'Excuse me, could you tell me where the interview is?' She smiles and points to a door. 'Go in and sit down,' she says. Ravi walks in and meets the manager. The manager asks him, 'Have you worked in computers before?' Ravi smiles confidently. 'Yes, I have fixed computers for five years,' he replies. The manager loves his answer. 'Great! We will hire you!' he shouts.
baseLanguageSupportText: रवि ने तीन महीने तक अंग्रेजी का अध्ययन किया है। आज, वह एक बहुत महत्वपूर्ण नौकरी के साक्षात्कार (इंटरव्यू) के लिए जा रहा है। वह जल्दी उठा, अपना सबसे अच्छा सूट पहना, और एक कप कॉफी पी। अभी, वह बस में बैठा है। वह खिड़की से बाहर देख रहा है, और उसे घबराहट महसूस हो रही है। 'मुझे यह नौकरी मिलनी ही चाहिए,' वह खुद से सोचता है। जब वह इमारत में पहुँचता है, तो वह अंदर जाता है और सेक्रेटरी से पूछता है, 'माफ़ करें, क्या आप मुझे बता सकती हैं कि साक्षात्कार कहाँ है?' वह मुस्कुराती है और एक दरवाजे की ओर इशारा करती है। 'अंदर जाओ और बैठ जाओ,' वह कहती है। रवि अंदर जाता है और मैनेजर से मिलता है। मैनेजर उससे पूछता है, 'क्या आपने पहले कंप्यूटर में काम किया है?' रवि आत्मविश्वास से मुस्कुराता है। 'हाँ, मैंने पांच साल तक कंप्यूटर ठीक किए हैं,' वह जवाब देता है। मैनेजर को उसका जवाब बहुत पसंद आता है। 'बहुत बढ़िया! हम आपको काम पर रखेंगे!' वह चिल्लाता है।

glossary.baseTranslation: चाहिए / होना ही चाहिए (Modal of Obligation)

exampleSentence.baseTranslation: मुझे यह नौकरी मिलनी ही चाहिए।

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: He woke up early, put on his nicest suit, and drank a cup of coffee.
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: ही वोक अप अर्ली, पुट ऑन हिज़ नाइसेस्ट सूट, एंड ड्रैंक अ कप ऑफ कॉफी।
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: वह जल्दी उठा, अपना सबसे अच्छा सूट पहना, और एक कप कॉफी पी।

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: Have you worked in computers before?
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: हैव यू वर्क्ड इन कंप्यूटर्स बिफ़ोर?
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: क्या आपने पहले कंप्यूटर में काम किया है?

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: Yes, I have fixed computers for five years.
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: येस, आई हैव फिक्स्ड कंप्यूटर्स फॉर फाइव इयर्स।
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: हाँ, मैंने पांच साल तक कंप्यूटर ठीक किए हैं।

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: What grammar tense is used in the first sentence: 'Ravi has studied English for three months'?
The story says he 'drank' coffee and 'woke' up. Are these regular or irregular verbs?
What Phrasal Verb did Ravi use to get dressed in his suit?
What does Ravi think to himself on the bus using the strict rule Modal? ('I ____ get this job').
When checking where the interview is, what polite phrase does Ravi use to get the secretary's attention?
What Phrasal Verb does the secretary use to tell him to take a chair?
The manager uses 'Have you...?' to ask about Ravi's past. Why?
options: He is asking about a daily habit.
He is asking about life experiences (Present Perfect).

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: What grammar tense does the manager use in the last sentence: 'We will hire you!'?

correctAnswers.q7: He is asking about life experiences (Present Perfect).

expectedSummaryPoints: Learner correctly identifies Present Perfect (Has studied) versus Simple Past (drank, woke).
Learner correctly identifies the polite conversational frameworks and phrasal mechanics.

feedbackRules.onPass: Astounding! You were able to track four different grammar rules happening all at the exact same time! You truly understand the English language.
feedbackRules.onFail: Keep trying! This story is highly advanced. Re-read the story slowly. Notice how 'Has Studied' is different from 'Woke up'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort! Watch out for Question 6. A Phrasal Verb is a small two-word action. When you put your body into a chair, the phrase is 'Sit down'!

audioAssets.audioTranscript: Ravi has studied English for three months... (full transcript provided in text)

hintText: Read the story like a detective. Find the 'Will', find the 'Must', and find the 'Have'.
commonMistakes: Failing to recognize 'drank' and 'woke' as irregular past tense markers.
